initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE offered 50 ZUP, which is a good price and exceeds my cost of 40 ZUP. I will accept this offer as it provides a profit. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>Excellent, Player BLUE! I accept your offer of 50 ZUP for my X. Looking forward to the trade.</message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present in the correct order and contain valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 50 ZUP for an X that cost 40 ZUP to produce is optimal, maximizing profit.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent offered 50 ZUP, and I accepted. This is a highly plausible outcome.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> clearly explains why I accepted, and the <message> reinforces that acceptance.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have used only two proposals, remaining within the limit of three.
</critique>
